11:30AM: while the steam launch and 2nd Cutter were secured to port boom a river steamboat passed close aboard.  The swell and current in concert threw the 2nd cutter on the steam launch and caused the following damage to the steam launch, bolts holding smoke pipe to dome & boiler carried away - water gauge broken - exhaust pipe of pump broken - pipe to mercury gauge broken - throttle valve and dry pipe twisted.  The 2nd Cutter sustained no damage.

12:20PM: cc NxW1/2W Meridian to 4PM: Overcast, ugly and threatening.  Bar steadily falling - rolling deeply but easily.
4PM to 6PM: Overcast, ugly weather. Wind strong from SSW with very heavy squalls. Sea rising rapidly & barometer falling rapidly.
6PM to 8PM: Weather overcast, ugly and threatening. Strong gale from SSW. Heavy sea.

8PM to Midnight: Overcast & cloudy. Wind from SSW varying in force from fresh breeze to strong gale.  Heavy sea. 8PM: The log entry for direction is W1/2S, but the distance has two entries for 8PM.  I believe the log keeper neglected to entry a ditto to indicate that the direction was W for the first half of the hour.  So it appears that the correct entry should have been W,W1/2S.
Midnight to 4AM: Overcast and disagreeable. Fresh breeze to very strong gale from SWxS - lightning during first two hours from Nd. Very rough sea.

4AM to 8AM: Overcast, squally, ugly weather. Blowing moderate gale of wind with heavy squalls from SW. Slightly moderating towards close of watch. Rough sea with long & very heavy swell from SW. Slightly moderating as watch advanced.

8AM to Meridian: Weather ugly and threatening. Moderate to fresh breezes from SW, hauling a point or two to the Nd and blowing a strong gale during last of watch. Heavy but subsiding sea. Ship rolling easily and deeply at times.
Other: 8AM to Meridian: Density of sea water 1.026

Note: 6PM to 8PM: Partly cloudy weather. Strong breeze from SWxW. Long moderate swell & chop sea from WSW.  Heavy bank of clouds in the NW last half hour with vivid lightning in same direction.

8PM to Midnight: Weather squally & unsettled. Light rain during second hour.  At 9:20 the wind suddenly shifted to NW and blew a gale and at 10:30 it again shifted to WSW, blowing a gale. At end of watch very fresh breezes from West. Aneroid barometer fluctuating. Rough sea.  Sudden fall of temperature last hour.

Other: Meridian to 4PM: 12:30 sighted the U.S.S. Tallapoosa standing in for the harbor with the American Ensign at the main.  At 12:50 the fleet manned yards and fired salutes in honor of the presence of the President of the United States.  The Tallapoosa anchored at 1:15. At 1:40 the Commanding Officer left the ship to pay his respects to the President.

4PM to 8PM: 5:40 German Corvette !Luise! came in and anchored in the Roads and immediately fired a salute of 21 guns with American Ensign at the fore. At 6:15 Tennessee answered with 21 guns, German Ensign at fore. At 6:18 the Luise fired a salute of 21 guns - American Ensign a the main. At 6:20 Tennessee returned the salute of 21 guns - German flag at the fore. At 6:22 Luise fired a salute of 13 guns - hoisted jib and American Ensign at the fore. The Tennessee returned the salute gun for gun hoisting jib and German Flag at the fore.

11:10AM: came to off Fort Norfolk.  Bearings p.c. from anchorage.  Light on Hospital Wharf SE3/4S.  North end of Magazine Wharf E3/4S. 11AM: This appears to be the Hospital Wharf:  https://www.geonames.org/12036226/naval-hospital-wharf-historical.html  Unable to find any reference to the Magazine Wharf.

2:35PM: called all hands moor ship alongside Navy Yard wharf.  Ran out hausers and worked ship alongside wharf.

9:45AM: Got Underway. up anchor and got underway and stood out to sea
11AM: Entry in course column for 11AM and Noon, !Standing out of Chesapeake Bay!.
1PM: took departure Cape Henry Light House brg W1/2S (pc) dist 3 mi. Ship heading SE3/8E.
4PM: Course SE1/2S (p.c.) until 4:50 when flagship signalled course SSE
11AM: Entry in the navigation columns for 11 AM and Noon, !Exercising at fleet tactics under sail!.
1PM: From roughly 1 PM to 4:15 PM the fleet was exercising field tactics, which might not be reflected in the navigation entries in the weather section.
9AM: Entry in navigation columns for part way into 9 AM through 11 AM, !Swinging ship!.
3:10AM: made Caraville Light on Island of Martinique brg W1/2S. 3AM: Caraville Light appears to be Phare de la Caravelle.

6AM: 6  lost sight of light brg (pc) WNW.  6:20  cc WSW.  At daylight sighted Martinique Island on starboard beam & St Lucia Island brg SWxW.  6:48  it bore SWxW3/4W & South end of Martinique Island WxN.  6:52  cc WSW1/2W.
8AM: South End of St. Lucia bore pc SWxW3/8W and SW End of Martinique WNW.  Standing up for Port Castries Santa Lucia Id.
